questions.q: What record did NASA’s Artemis II astronauts break on April 6, 2026?
options: The record for the longest spacewalk.
The record for the fastest spacecraft.
The record for the farthest distance traveled from Earth by humans.
The record for the most days spent on the moon.
questions.correctText: The Artemis II astronauts officially became the farthest humans from Earth, surpassing the record set by Apollo 13 in 1970.

questions.q: Which previous mission's distance record did the Artemis II astronauts surpass on April 6, 2026?
questions.correctText: On April 6, 2026, the Artemis II crew exceeded the distance record held by the Apollo 13 crew for 56 years.

questions.q: On April 6, 2026, what did Iran reject in relation to the Strait of Hormuz?
options: A temporary ceasefire to reopen the Strait.
A joint military exercise with the US.
questions.correctText: Iran rejected a proposal for a temporary ceasefire to reopen the Strait of Hormuz on April 6, 2026.

questions.q: What was Iran's primary demand in response to the ceasefire proposal regarding the Strait of Hormuz on April 6, 2026?
options: A permanent end to the war.
The immediate lifting of all international sanctions.
The release of all Iranian prisoners.
questions.correctText: Iran's primary demand in response to the ceasefire proposal was a permanent end to the war and a protocol for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

questions.q: On April 6, 2026, Steve Bannon won a Supreme Court order that is likely to lead to the dismissal of his conviction for what crime?
questions.correctText: Steve Bannon won a Supreme Court order that is expected to lead to the dismissal of his criminal conviction for contempt of Congress.
